You're meant to do something. You're not just meant to lie there in the sand and meditate all day long. So you should do what you're meant to do. But the idea that you're going to change something in the outside world that is going to bring you the peace and everlasting joy and happiness that you deserve—that is a fundamental delusion that we all suffer from, including me. And so the mistake over and over and over is to say, 'Oh, I'll be happy when I get that thing,' whatever that is. That's the fundamental mistake that we all make, including me, 24/7, all day long.
turns.zh: 我认为最常见的错误——这个问题很难回答，因为太依赖情境了。真的要看情况。如果我看地球上的人类，最常见的错误是认为某种外部环境会让你幸福。我知道这不新鲜，这是基本的佛教智慧，我不居功。但我现在真的从根本层面认识到了，包括我自己。我们刚买了辆新车，因为有了孩子，需要更安全的车。之前开的是 Mini Cooper，空间不够。所以买了新车，现在我在等车到货。当然，每晚我都在论坛上看关于这辆车的帖子。我为什么这么做？是因为这个物体吗？流线型的车？它不会改变我的生活太多。而且我知道车一到，我就不会再关心它了。但事实是，我上瘾于渴望。我上瘾于这种想法：这个外部的东西会给我带来某种幸福。这完全是妄想。所以我认为向外寻求任何东西都是根本的妄想。我不是说你什么都不该做。你绝对应该做。你是一个生物。你做事情来局部逆转熵。这就是你在这里的原因。你注定要做些什么。你不是注定躺在沙滩上整天冥想。所以你应该做你该做的事。但认为改变外部世界就能带来你应得的平静、永恒快乐和幸福——这是我们所有人都有的根本妄想，包括我。所以一次又一次的错误就是说‘等我得到那个东西，我就幸福了’，不管那是什么。这是我们所有人犯的根本错误，包括我，一天 24 小时，每时每刻。

turns.en: I definitely see myself in that answer because you're human. You're human, all of us do it. I want to end with a really unbounded big question, which is: what is the meaning and purpose of life?
turns.zh: 我从这个回答中看到了自己，因为你是人。你是人，我们都会这样。我想以一个无边无际的大问题结束：生命的意义和目的是什么？

turns.en: Yeah, it's a big question. I'll give you three answers because it's a big question. One: it's personal. You have to find your own meaning. Any piece of wisdom that anybody else gives you, whether it's Buddha or you or me, it's going to sound like nonsense. So I think fundamentally you just have to find it for yourself. So the important part is not the answer, it's the question. You have to sit there and dig with the question. It might take you years or decades, but when you find an answer you're happy with, that will be fundamental to your life. The second answer I would give is: there is no meaning to life, there's no purpose to life. Osho says it's like riding on water or building houses in sand. The reality is you've been dead for the history of the universe—ten billion years or more—and you will be for the next 70 billion years or so until the heat death of the universe. And anything you do will fade, it will disappear. The human race will disappear, the planet will disappear, my leaving that group will disappear. So no one's going to remember you past a certain number of generations, whether you're an artist or a poet or a conqueror or a pauper or nothing. So there's no meaning. So you have to create your own meaning, which is what about until you have to decide: is it a play that I'm going through that I'm just watching? Is there a self-actualization dance that I'm doing? Is there a specific thing that I just desire just for the heck of it? But these are all meanings you are making up. There's no fundamental intrinsic purposeful meaning to the universe, because if there was, then you would just ask the next question yourself: 'Well, why is that the meaning?' So we just be, as Richard Feynman said, it'd be 'turtles all the way down.' The wise just keep accumulating. There is no answer you can give to that question that wouldn't have another 'why.' And I don't buy the everlasting afterlife answers, because it's insane to me, with absolutely no evidence, to believe that because of how you live 70 years here on this planet, you then spend an eternity—which is a very long time—in some afterlife. What kind of silly God judges you for eternity based on some small period of time here? So I think after this life, it's very much like before you were born. Remember that? It's going to be just like that. And before you were born, you didn't care about anything or anyone, including your loved ones, including yourself, including humans, including whether we go to Mars or whether we stay on planet Earth, and whether there's an AI or not. You just don't care. I met this entrepreneur who was obsessed with Steve Jobs and he was making all these sacrifices trying to be like the next Steve Jobs. And I said, 'Tim, do you want to be exactly like Steve Jobs right now?' He said yes. So Steve Jobs is dead. He doesn't care about anything. He's got zero. He's not registering at all. So if you want to be like Steve Jobs, you want to be dead? That's not a good goal.
turns.zh: 是的，这是个很大的问题。因为问题大，我给你三个答案。第一：这是个人的。你必须找到自己的意义。别人给你的任何智慧，无论是佛陀、你还是我，听起来都会像废话。所以我认为你基本上必须自己找到。所以重要的不是答案，而是问题。你必须坐下来，深入挖掘这个问题。可能需要几年或几十年，但当你找到一个让你满意的答案时，那将成为你人生的基石。我要给的第二个答案是：生命没有意义，没有目的。奥修说这就像在水上骑行或在沙上建房子。现实是，你在宇宙历史中已经死了——100 亿年或更久——你还会再死大约 700 亿年，直到宇宙热寂。你做的任何事都会褪色、消失。人类会消失，地球会消失，我离开那个群体也会消失。所以无论你是艺术家、诗人、征服者、穷人还是无名小卒，几代之后没人会记得你。所以没有意义。你必须创造自己的意义，这取决于你决定：这是我在经历的一场戏，我只是在观看？是我在做的自我实现之舞？还是我纯粹因为喜欢而渴望的某件事？但这些意义都是你编造的。宇宙没有根本的内在目的性意义，因为如果有，你就会问下一个问题：‘为什么那是意义？’所以我们只是存在，就像理查德·费曼说的，‘全是乌龟，一路向下。’智者只是不断积累。对于那个问题，你给出的任何答案都会引出另一个‘为什么’。我不相信永生的来世答案，因为在我看来，毫无证据地相信你在地球上活 70 年，然后就能在某个来世度过永恒——那是非常长的时间——这很荒谬。什么样的愚蠢上帝会根据你在这里的短暂时间来永恒地审判你？所以我认为死后就像你出生前一样。还记得吗？就会像那样。你出生前，不关心任何事或任何人，包括你的亲人、你自己、人类、我们是否去火星、是否留在地球、是否有 AI。你根本不在乎。我遇到一个企业家，他痴迷于史蒂夫·乔布斯，做出各种牺牲想成为下一个乔布斯。我说：‘蒂姆，你现在想完全像史蒂夫·乔布斯一样吗？’他说想。但史蒂夫·乔布斯死了。他什么都不在乎。他完全归零。他没有任何感知。所以如果你想成为乔布斯，你想死？那不是个好目标。

turns.en: You want to be Mike's new job? You don't want to be like Steve Jobs. Just be you. Right now he would trade places with you in an instant if he could. So I don't think there's any real meaning or purpose to life. And in the last answer I'll give you is a little more complicated than that. From what I've been reading in science, friends of mine have written books on this, a kind of stitched together theories, and maybe there is a meaning to life, but it's not a very satisfying purpose. Basically in physics, the arrow of time comes from entropy. The second law of thermodynamics is that entropy only goes up, which means disorder in the universe only goes up, which means concentrated free energy only goes down. And if you look at what living things are—living systems, humans, plants, civilizations, what have you—they are systems that are locally reversing entropy. Humans locally reverse entropy because we have action, but in the process we globally accelerate entropy until the heat death of the universe. So you could come up with some fanciful theory, which I have, which I like, that we're headed towards a heat death of the universe where there's no concentrated energy, where everything is sort of at the same energy level, and therefore we're all one thing, we're essentially indistinguishable. And what we are doing as living systems is we're accelerating getting to that state. So the more complex a system you create, whether it's through computers and civilization or through art and mathematics or just creating a family, you're actually accelerating the heat death of the universe, so you're pushing us to this point where we end up as one thing. But I think that's kind of an unsatisfying answer if you're looking for personal meaning today in your life.
turns.zh: 你想成为迈克的新工作吗？你不想成为史蒂夫·乔布斯那样的人。做你自己就好。如果他能的话，他现在会立刻和你交换位置。所以我不认为生命有任何真正的意义或目的。我给你的最后一个答案会稍微复杂一点。根据我读到的科学内容，我的一些朋友写过这方面的书，拼凑了一些理论，也许生命是有意义的，但这不是一个非常令人满意的目的。基本上在物理学中，时间之箭来自熵。热力学第二定律说熵只会增加，这意味着宇宙中的无序只会增加，这意味着集中的自由能只会减少。如果你看看生物是什么——生命系统、人类、植物、文明等等——它们是局部逆转熵的系统。人类局部逆转熵是因为我们有行动，但在这个过程中，我们全局加速熵，直到宇宙热寂。所以你可以提出一些奇妙的理论，我也有一个我喜欢的理论：我们正走向宇宙热寂，那里没有集中的能量，一切都处于相同的能量水平，因此我们都是一体的，本质上无法区分。而我们作为生命系统所做的，就是加速达到那个状态。所以你创造的系统越复杂，无论是通过计算机和文明，还是通过艺术和数学，或者仅仅是组建家庭，你实际上都在加速宇宙的热寂，所以你正在推动我们走向最终成为一体的那个点。但如果你在寻找今天生活中的个人意义，我认为这是一个不太令人满意的答案。

turns.en: That was phenomenal. Listen, thank you so much. This was just absolutely mind-blowing. I really appreciate it. Where can people find you?
turns.zh: 太棒了。听着，非常感谢你。这真是令人难以置信。我真的很感激。人们在哪里可以找到你？

turns.en: Twitter at @naval, or on my blog at startupboy.com. And thank you so much. Thank you, it's been great.
turns.zh: Twitter 上 @naval，或者我的博客 startupboy.com。非常感谢你。谢谢，这很棒。

turns.en: Hey guys, this is Shane again. Just a few more things before we wrap up. You can find show notes at farnamstreetblog.com slash podcast. That's F-A-R-N-A-M-S-T-R-E-E-T-B-L-O-G dot com slash podcast. You can also find information there on how to get a transcript. And if you'd like to receive a weekly email from me filled with all sorts of brain food, go to farnamstreetblog.com slash newsletter. This is all the good stuff I found on the web that week that I've read and shared with close friends, books I'm reading, and so much more. Thank you for listening.
turns.zh: 嘿，大家好，我是 Shane。在我们结束之前还有几件事。你可以在 farnamstreetblog.com/podcast 找到节目笔记。那是 F-A-R-N-A-M-S-T-R-E-E-T-B-L-O-G 点 com 斜杠 podcast。你还可以在那里找到如何获取文字记录的信息。如果你想每周收到我的一封邮件，里面充满各种精神食粮，请访问 farnamstreetblog.com/newsletter。这是我那周在网上找到的所有好东西，我读过并与密友分享的，我正在读的书，以及更多。感谢收听。
